title: CompassTrack
description: Track players in real time with your compass — ideal for PvP, manhunts, and survival. Includes cooldowns, permissions, and live updates.
body:
🧭 CompassTrack

CompassTrack is a lightweight yet powerful Minecraft plugin that lets players track other players using a compass — perfect for PvP, hunting events, or survival adventures!

✨ Features

/track <player> – Start tracking another player with your compass.
The compass will automatically point to the target player's current position.
You must have a compass in your inventory for tracking to work.

/track off – Stops tracking.
Your compass will no longer point to any player.

Automatic Compass Updates –
While tracking is active, your compass continuously updates as the target moves.

Cooldown System –
The /track command can only be used every 30 seconds by default to prevent spam.
If you try to use it too early, you’ll receive a message showing how long you need to wait.

🔒 Permissions
Permission	Description	Default
compassTrack.use	Allows use of the /track command	OP only
compassTrack.bypasscooldown	Allows bypassing the cooldown	OP only
compassTrack.trackable	Only players with this permission can be tracked	Everyone
⚙️ Compatibility

Works with Paper, Spigot, and Bukkit

Supported Versions: 1.20 – 1.21+

API Version: 1.20

💬 Feedback Messages

The plugin gives clear messages when:

Tracking starts or stops

You don’t have a compass

The cooldown is active

Tracking is successful

🧠 Perfect for

PvP & survival servers

Hunter vs. hunted events

Team or clan gameplay
